GHOST Document Retrieval and Reranking Requirements
Document
  • VIEWS
  • Document
  • Table
  • Traceability
  • Deep Traceability

    • GHOST Document Retrieval and Reranking Requirements

    UID: GHOST-DOCUMENT-RETRIEVAL-RERANKING
    VERSION: 0.1

    1. 1. Purpose and Scope

    STATEMENT: This document defines query-time document retrieval and optional reranking within GHOST Knowledge Management.
    GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-ROOT
    1.1. Document Retrieval and Reranking Scope
    UID: GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-ROOT
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor
    RELATIONS (Child):
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-PRODUCT-NAME Product and Package Naming Boundary
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-KM-PARENT Retrieval Knowledge Management Parent
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-BOUNDARY Retrieval Boundary
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-COMPONENTS Current Retrieval Components
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-SP SuperPrompt Input
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-QUERY-SPLIT Query Piece Generation
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-DENSE-FIRST Dense First-Pass Retrieval
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-SPARSE-OPTION SPLADE Enable Switch
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-RRF1 Retrieval RRF Fusion
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-HYDRATE Hydration Requirement
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-WRITE Retrieval Write-Back
    • GHOST-DOC-RERANK-ROOT Reranker Capability
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-CONFIG-JSON JSON Configuration Target
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-EFFECTIVE Effective Retrieval Diagnostics
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-ERROR Retrieval Error Integrity
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-OPTIONAL-ERROR Optional Branch Failure Boundary
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-LOG-CONTENT Retrieval Logging Content
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-CODE-OWNERSHIP Retrieval Code Ownership
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-SIBLING Sibling Ownership Boundary
    STATEMENT: GHOST shall retrieve query-relevant document chunks from an active project, optionally apply SPLADE and ColBERT ranking branches, hydrate selected chunks from raw sources, and write ordered candidate state into SuperPrompt for A3 evidence selection.
    GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-PRODUCT-NAME
    1.2. Product and Package Naming Boundary
    UID: GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-PRODUCT-NAME
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-ROOT Document Retrieval and Reranking Scope
    STATEMENT: The product described by this document shall be named GHOST; current Python paths may remain under ragstream.
    GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-KM-PARENT
    1.3. Retrieval Knowledge Management Parent
    UID: GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-KM-PARENT
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-ROOT Document Retrieval and Reranking Scope
    STATEMENT: Document Retrieval and Reranking shall be governed by the capability boundaries, shared artifacts, configuration governance, and sibling ownership defined in 40_knowledge_management.sdoc.

    This document shall refine the retrieval/reranking sub-capability without redefining ingestion, A3, A4, or Memory Management.
    GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-BOUNDARY
    1.4. Retrieval Boundary
    UID: GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-BOUNDARY
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-ROOT Document Retrieval and Reranking Scope
    STATEMENT: This document shall own query projection consumption, query splitting, dense ranking, SPLADE rescoring, reciprocal-rank fusion, hydration, retrieval-stage write-back, optional ColBERT reranking, and reranked-stage write-back.

    It shall not own ingestion, A3 usefulness decisions, A4 condensation, memory retrieval, or final prompt composition.
    GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-COMPONENTS
    1.5. Current Retrieval Components
    UID: GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-COMPONENTS
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-ROOT Document Retrieval and Reranking Scope
    STATEMENT: The current capability shall be organized around Retriever, RetrieverEmb, RetrieverSplade, rrf_merge, smart_query_splitter, Chunk hydration, and Reranker.

    2. 2. Retrieval Input and Query Projection

    STATEMENT: Retrieval consumes the prepared SuperPrompt rather than reparsing raw input.
    GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-SP
    2.1. SuperPrompt Input
    UID: GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-SP
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-ROOT Document Retrieval and Reranking Scope
    RELATIONS (Child):
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-QUERY-PROJECTOR Query Projection Source
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-PROJECT Project Name
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-TOPK Requested Candidate Limit
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-NO-MEMORY-QUERY No Memory Query Ownership
    STATEMENT: Retriever shall receive the active prepared SuperPrompt, active project name, requested top_k, and SPLADE enable/bypass choice.
    GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-QUERY-PROJECTOR
    2.2. Query Projection Source
    UID: GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-QUERY-PROJECTOR
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-SP SuperPrompt Input
    RELATIONS (Child):
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-ROUTING ActiveBrief Routing Compliance
    STATEMENT: Retriever shall obtain document query text through SuperPromptProjector.build_query_text.
    GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-ROUTING
    2.3. ActiveBrief Routing Compliance
    UID: GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-ROUTING
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-QUERY-PROJECTOR Query Projection Source
    RELATIONS (Child):
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-EMPTY-SUPPRESSION Empty Query Suppression
    STATEMENT: The query text used for retrieval shall reflect the finalized prompt-materiality/topic-relation policy established by PreProcessing.
    GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-EMPTY-SUPPRESSION
    2.4. Empty Query Suppression
    UID: GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-EMPTY-SUPPRESSION
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-ROUTING ActiveBrief Routing Compliance
    STATEMENT: A deliberately empty document query produced by the routing policy shall suppress document retrieval and shall not be replaced by prompt_ready, memory context, or an unrelated fallback.
    GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-PROJECT
    2.5. Project Name
    UID: GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-PROJECT
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-SP SuperPrompt Input
    STATEMENT: The project name shall be non-empty and shall identify one project-specific dense store and raw-document subtree.
    GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-TOPK
    2.6. Requested Candidate Limit
    UID: GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-TOPK
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-SP SuperPrompt Input
    STATEMENT: The requested top_k shall define the maximum fused retrieval rows returned by the retrieval stage when positive.
    GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-NO-MEMORY-QUERY
    2.7. No Memory Query Ownership
    UID: GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-NO-MEMORY-QUERY
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-SP SuperPrompt Input
    STATEMENT: Document Retriever shall not query the memory vector store or MemoryIndexLookup.

    3. 3. Deterministic Query Splitting

    STATEMENT: The current smart_query_splitter is a deterministic linear overlapping splitter, not yet a semantic splitter.
    GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-QUERY-SPLIT
    3.1. Query Piece Generation
    UID: GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-QUERY-SPLIT
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-ROOT Document Retrieval and Reranking Scope
    RELATIONS (Child):
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-QUERY-TRIM Query Trim
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-QUERY-CHUNKER Shared Chunker Use
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-QUERY-SIZE Query Window Validation
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-QUERY-BASELINE Current Query Window Baseline
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-QUERY-ORDER Query Piece Order
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-NO-SMART-CLAIM No Semantic Splitter Claim
    STATEMENT: The projected query shall be split into ordered overlapping text pieces before dense and sparse scoring.
    GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-QUERY-TRIM
    3.2. Query Trim
    UID: GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-QUERY-TRIM
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-QUERY-SPLIT Query Piece Generation
    STATEMENT: Query splitting shall trim the complete query and shall return an empty list when no usable text remains.
    GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-QUERY-CHUNKER
    3.3. Shared Chunker Use
    UID: GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-QUERY-CHUNKER
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-QUERY-SPLIT Query Piece Generation
    STATEMENT: The current query splitter shall reuse the deterministic Chunker windowing algorithm.
    GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-QUERY-SIZE
    3.4. Query Window Validation
    UID: GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-QUERY-SIZE
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-QUERY-SPLIT Query Piece Generation
    STATEMENT: Query chunk size shall be positive; overlap shall be non-negative and smaller than chunk size.
    GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-QUERY-BASELINE
    3.5. Current Query Window Baseline
    UID: GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-QUERY-BASELINE
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-QUERY-SPLIT Query Piece Generation
    STATEMENT: The current query-splitting baseline shall use 1200-character windows with 120-character overlap until replaced by approved runtime configuration.
    GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-QUERY-ORDER
    3.6. Query Piece Order
    UID: GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-QUERY-ORDER
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-QUERY-SPLIT Query Piece Generation
    STATEMENT: Returned query pieces shall preserve the order produced by deterministic windowing and shall exclude empty pieces.
    GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-NO-SMART-CLAIM
    3.7. No Semantic Splitter Claim
    UID: GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-NO-SMART-CLAIM
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-QUERY-SPLIT Query Piece Generation
    STATEMENT: The current smart_query_splitter module shall not be described as semantic, sentence-aware, or LLM-based while its implementation remains linear character windowing.

    4. 4. Dense Candidate Generation

    STATEMENT: Dense retrieval is the mandatory first candidate-generation branch.
    GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-DENSE-FIRST
    4.1. Dense First-Pass Retrieval
    UID: GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-DENSE-FIRST
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-ROOT Document Retrieval and Reranking Scope
    RELATIONS (Child):
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-DENSE-NEUTRAL Dense Backend State Neutrality
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-DENSE-STORE Dense Store Validation
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-DENSE-EMPTY-QUERY Dense Empty Query
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-DENSE-READ Dense Store Read Contract
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-DENSE-LENGTH Dense Store Shape Validation
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-DENSE-QUERY-EMBED Query Embedding
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-DENSE-DIM Embedding Dimension Validation
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-COSINE Cosine Similarity
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-PNORM Query-Piece Aggregation
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-DENSE-SORT Dense Deterministic Sort
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-DENSE-CUTOFF Dense Candidate Cutoff
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-EMB-FLOOR Hard Dense Similarity Floor
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-AC-DENSE AC: Dense Ranking
    STATEMENT: RetrieverEmb shall generate the initial candidate set from the active project Chroma document store.
    GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-DENSE-NEUTRAL
    4.2. Dense Backend State Neutrality
    UID: GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-DENSE-NEUTRAL
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-DENSE-FIRST Dense First-Pass Retrieval
    STATEMENT: RetrieverEmb shall receive project name, query pieces, and top_k without knowledge of SuperPrompt write-back or raw-file hydration.
    GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-DENSE-STORE
    4.3. Dense Store Validation
    UID: GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-DENSE-STORE
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-DENSE-FIRST Dense First-Pass Retrieval
    STATEMENT: Dense retrieval shall fail explicitly when the Chroma root or active project store does not exist.
    GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-DENSE-EMPTY-QUERY
    4.4. Dense Empty Query
    UID: GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-DENSE-EMPTY-QUERY
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-DENSE-FIRST Dense First-Pass Retrieval
    STATEMENT: Dense retrieval shall return no rows when query pieces are empty.
    GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-DENSE-READ
    4.5. Dense Store Read Contract
    UID: GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-DENSE-READ
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-DENSE-FIRST Dense First-Pass Retrieval
    STATEMENT: Dense retrieval shall read stored chunk identifiers, embeddings, and metadata from the active project collection.
    GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-DENSE-LENGTH
    4.6. Dense Store Shape Validation
    UID: GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-DENSE-LENGTH
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-DENSE-FIRST Dense First-Pass Retrieval
    STATEMENT: Dense retrieval shall reject mismatched identifier/embedding lengths and mismatched non-empty metadata lengths.
    GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-DENSE-QUERY-EMBED
    4.7. Query Embedding
    UID: GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-DENSE-QUERY-EMBED
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-DENSE-FIRST Dense First-Pass Retrieval
    STATEMENT: The dense backend shall embed all query pieces using the configured dense Embedder.
    GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-DENSE-DIM
    4.8. Embedding Dimension Validation
    UID: GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-DENSE-DIM
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-DENSE-FIRST Dense First-Pass Retrieval
    STATEMENT: Stored and query embedding matrices shall be two-dimensional and shall have equal vector dimensions.
    GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-COSINE
    4.9. Cosine Similarity
    UID: GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-COSINE
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-DENSE-FIRST Dense First-Pass Retrieval
    STATEMENT: The dense backend shall row-normalize stored and query vectors and shall compute cosine similarities by matrix multiplication.
    GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-PNORM
    4.10. Query-Piece Aggregation
    UID: GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-PNORM
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-DENSE-FIRST Dense First-Pass Retrieval
    RELATIONS (Child):
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-PNORM-P Current Dense p Value
    STATEMENT: Per-chunk dense similarities across query pieces shall be clipped at zero and aggregated by p-norm mean.
    GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-PNORM-P
    4.11. Current Dense p Value
    UID: GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-PNORM-P
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-PNORM Query-Piece Aggregation
    STATEMENT: The current dense p-norm aggregation baseline shall use p = 10 until replaced by approved configuration.
    GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-DENSE-SORT
    4.12. Dense Deterministic Sort
    UID: GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-DENSE-SORT
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-DENSE-FIRST Dense First-Pass Retrieval
    STATEMENT: Dense rows shall be ordered by descending aggregated score and then ascending chunk identifier for ties.
    GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-DENSE-CUTOFF
    4.13. Dense Candidate Cutoff
    UID: GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-DENSE-CUTOFF
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-DENSE-FIRST Dense First-Pass Retrieval
    STATEMENT: Dense retrieval shall return no more than the positive requested top_k or its documented fallback when the caller supplies no valid positive limit.

    5. 5. Hard Embedding Floor

    STATEMENT: The dense result is filtered before SPLADE scoring and first fusion.
    GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-EMB-FLOOR
    5.1. Hard Dense Similarity Floor
    UID: GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-EMB-FLOOR
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-DENSE-FIRST Dense First-Pass Retrieval
    RELATIONS (Child):
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-EMB-FLOOR-ZERO Disabled Floor
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-EMB-FLOOR-SOURCE Floor Configuration Source
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-EMB-FLOOR-META Floor Score Meaning
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-AC-FLOOR AC: Hard Floor
    STATEMENT: Retriever shall remove dense candidate rows whose dense score is below the configured hard_embedding_floor.
    GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-EMB-FLOOR-ZERO
    5.2. Disabled Floor
    UID: GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-EMB-FLOOR-ZERO
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-EMB-FLOOR Hard Dense Similarity Floor
    STATEMENT: A non-positive hard_embedding_floor shall disable dense-floor filtering.
    GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-EMB-FLOOR-SOURCE
    5.3. Floor Configuration Source
    UID: GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-EMB-FLOOR-SOURCE
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-EMB-FLOOR Hard Dense Similarity Floor
    STATEMENT: The current hard embedding floor shall be read from runtime_config.document_retrieval.hard_embedding_floor.
    GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-EMB-FLOOR-META
    5.4. Floor Score Meaning
    UID: GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-EMB-FLOOR-META
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-EMB-FLOOR Hard Dense Similarity Floor
    STATEMENT: The score compared to hard_embedding_floor shall be the aggregated dense embedding score later projected as emb_score.

    6. 6. Optional SPLADE Rescoring Branch

    STATEMENT: The current SPLADE branch rescoring scope is bounded by dense-selected candidate identifiers.
    GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-SPARSE-OPTION
    6.1. SPLADE Enable Switch
    UID: GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-SPARSE-OPTION
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-ROOT Document Retrieval and Reranking Scope
    RELATIONS (Child):
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-SPARSE-LAZY Lazy Sparse Initialization
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-SPARSE-CANDIDATES Dense-Bounded Sparse Candidates
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-SPARSE-STORE Sparse Store Validation
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-SPARSE-QUERY Query-Side Sparse Encoding
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-SPARSE-DOT Sparse Similarity
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-SPARSE-PNORM Sparse Piece Aggregation
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-SPARSE-SORT Sparse Deterministic Sort
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-SPARSE-MISSING-STORE Missing Sparse Store Fallback
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-SPARSE-BYPASS SPLADE Bypass Contract
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-SPARSE-BYPASS-DIAG SPLADE Bypass Observability
    STATEMENT: Retriever shall accept an explicit use_retrieval_splade switch.
    GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-SPARSE-LAZY
    6.2. Lazy Sparse Initialization
    UID: GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-SPARSE-LAZY
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-SPARSE-OPTION SPLADE Enable Switch
    STATEMENT: The SPLADE encoder and RetrieverSplade backend shall be initialized lazily on first enabled use.
    GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-SPARSE-CANDIDATES
    6.3. Dense-Bounded Sparse Candidates
    UID: GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-SPARSE-CANDIDATES
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-SPARSE-OPTION SPLADE Enable Switch
    RELATIONS (Child):
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-SPARSE-NOT-INDEPENDENT No Independent Sparse Search Claim
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-SPARSE-MISSING-ID Sparse Candidate Alignment
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-AC-SPARSE AC: Dense-Bounded SPLADE
    STATEMENT: When enabled, RetrieverSplade shall score exactly the unique ordered identifiers surviving dense retrieval and hard-floor filtering.
    GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-SPARSE-NOT-INDEPENDENT
    6.4. No Independent Sparse Search Claim
    UID: GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-SPARSE-NOT-INDEPENDENT
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-SPARSE-CANDIDATES Dense-Bounded Sparse Candidates
    STATEMENT: The current enabled SPLADE branch shall not be described as an independent global sparse candidate generator because it receives dense-selected candidate_ids.
    GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-SPARSE-STORE
    6.5. Sparse Store Validation
    UID: GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-SPARSE-STORE
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-SPARSE-OPTION SPLADE Enable Switch
    STATEMENT: Enabled sparse retrieval shall validate the SPLADE root and active project store.
    GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-SPARSE-MISSING-ID
    6.6. Sparse Candidate Alignment
    UID: GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-SPARSE-MISSING-ID
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-SPARSE-CANDIDATES Dense-Bounded Sparse Candidates
    STATEMENT: Every dense-selected candidate identifier shall exist in the active SPLADE store; missing identifiers shall fail the sparse backend explicitly.
    GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-SPARSE-QUERY
    6.7. Query-Side Sparse Encoding
    UID: GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-SPARSE-QUERY
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-SPARSE-OPTION SPLADE Enable Switch
    STATEMENT: RetrieverSplade shall encode query pieces using the query-side sparse encoder.
    GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-SPARSE-DOT
    6.8. Sparse Similarity
    UID: GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-SPARSE-DOT
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-SPARSE-OPTION SPLADE Enable Switch
    STATEMENT: Sparse document and query representations shall be compared by deterministic sparse dot product.
    GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-SPARSE-PNORM
    6.9. Sparse Piece Aggregation
    UID: GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-SPARSE-PNORM
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-SPARSE-OPTION SPLADE Enable Switch
    RELATIONS (Child):
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-SPARSE-P Current Sparse p Value
    STATEMENT: Per-chunk sparse similarities across query pieces shall be clipped at zero and aggregated by p-norm mean.
    GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-SPARSE-P
    6.10. Current Sparse p Value
    UID: GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-SPARSE-P
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-SPARSE-PNORM Sparse Piece Aggregation
    STATEMENT: The current sparse p-norm aggregation baseline shall use p = 10 until replaced by approved configuration.
    GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-SPARSE-SORT
    6.11. Sparse Deterministic Sort
    UID: GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-SPARSE-SORT
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-SPARSE-OPTION SPLADE Enable Switch
    STATEMENT: Sparse rows shall be sorted by descending aggregated score and ascending chunk identifier for ties.
    GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-SPARSE-MISSING-STORE
    6.12. Missing Sparse Store Fallback
    UID: GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-SPARSE-MISSING-STORE
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-SPARSE-OPTION SPLADE Enable Switch
    STATEMENT: When the top-level Retriever encounters FileNotFoundError for an older project without a SPLADE store, it shall continue with an empty sparse row list rather than aborting dense retrieval.
    GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-SPARSE-BYPASS
    6.13. SPLADE Bypass Contract
    UID: GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-SPARSE-BYPASS
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-SPARSE-OPTION SPLADE Enable Switch
    RELATIONS (Child):
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-AC-SPARSE-BYPASS AC: SPLADE Bypass
    STATEMENT: When SPLADE is disabled, Retriever shall duplicate the dense ranked rows into the second fusion input so the downstream fusion/write-back contract remains unchanged.
    GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-SPARSE-BYPASS-DIAG
    6.14. SPLADE Bypass Observability
    UID: GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-SPARSE-BYPASS-DIAG
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-SPARSE-OPTION SPLADE Enable Switch
    STATEMENT: The system shall expose whether SPLADE used real sparse scores, was unavailable, or was bypassed with dense duplication.

    7. 7. First Weighted Reciprocal Rank Fusion

    STATEMENT: Dense and sparse-ranked rows are fused by one neutral deterministic helper.
    GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-RRF1
    7.1. Retrieval RRF Fusion
    UID: GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-RRF1
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-ROOT Document Retrieval and Reranking Scope
    RELATIONS (Child):
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-RRF-K RRF Constant
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-RRF-WEIGHTS Current Retrieval Fusion Weights
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-RRF-SCORE RRF Score Calculation
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-RRF-UNION RRF Identifier Union
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-RRF-META RRF Metadata
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-RRF-META-MERGE Conservative Metadata Merge
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-RRF-SORT Fused Deterministic Sort
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-RRF-CUTOFF Fused Retrieval Cutoff
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-RRF-PROJECTION Retrieval Metadata Projection
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-AC-RRF1 AC: First Fusion
    STATEMENT: Retriever shall fuse dense and sparse/bypass ranked lists using weighted reciprocal-rank fusion.
    GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-RRF-K
    7.2. RRF Constant
    UID: GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-RRF-K
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-RRF1 Retrieval RRF Fusion
    STATEMENT: The RRF helper shall use a positive configurable rank constant; the current baseline shall be 60.
    GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-RRF-WEIGHTS
    7.3. Current Retrieval Fusion Weights
    UID: GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-RRF-WEIGHTS
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-RRF1 Retrieval RRF Fusion
    STATEMENT: The current retrieval fusion baseline shall weight the dense list 0.75 and the sparse/bypass list 0.25 until replaced by approved JSON configuration.
    GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-RRF-SCORE
    7.4. RRF Score Calculation
    UID: GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-RRF-SCORE
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-RRF1 Retrieval RRF Fusion
    STATEMENT: Each available rank contribution shall equal its branch weight divided by RRF constant plus one-based rank; contributions shall be summed by chunk identifier.
    GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-RRF-UNION
    7.5. RRF Identifier Union
    UID: GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-RRF-UNION
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-RRF1 Retrieval RRF Fusion
    STATEMENT: The neutral RRF helper shall support the union of identifiers appearing in either input list.
    GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-RRF-META
    7.6. RRF Metadata
    UID: GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-RRF-META
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-RRF1 Retrieval RRF Fusion
    STATEMENT: The neutral RRF result shall preserve merged metadata and attach generic branch scores, branch ranks, and fused rrf_score when available.
    GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-RRF-META-MERGE
    7.7. Conservative Metadata Merge
    UID: GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-RRF-META-MERGE
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-RRF1 Retrieval RRF Fusion
    STATEMENT: Metadata fusion shall retain an existing key value and shall add only keys absent from the accumulated metadata.
    GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-RRF-SORT
    7.8. Fused Deterministic Sort
    UID: GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-RRF-SORT
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-RRF1 Retrieval RRF Fusion
    STATEMENT: Fused rows shall be ordered by descending fused score and ascending chunk identifier for ties.
    GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-RRF-CUTOFF
    7.9. Fused Retrieval Cutoff
    UID: GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-RRF-CUTOFF
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-RRF1 Retrieval RRF Fusion
    STATEMENT: The retrieval RRF output shall be limited to the requested positive top_k.
    GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-RRF-PROJECTION
    7.10. Retrieval Metadata Projection
    UID: GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-RRF-PROJECTION
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-RRF1 Retrieval RRF Fusion
    STATEMENT: Retriever shall map neutral RRF branch fields to retrieval-specific emb_score, splade_score, emb_rank, splade_rank, and rrf_score aliases without discarding neutral metadata.

    8. 8. Source Hydration and Chunk Reconstruction

    STATEMENT: Vector stores hold vectors and metadata; raw chunk text is reconstructed from doc_raw.
    GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-HYDRATE
    8.1. Hydration Requirement
    UID: GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-HYDRATE
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-ROOT Document Retrieval and Reranking Scope
    RELATIONS (Child):
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-HYDRATE-PATH Hydration Source Path
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-HYDRATE-INDEX Hydration Chunk Index
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-HYDRATE-SAME-CONFIG Hydration Chunking Consistency
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-HYDRATE-CACHE Per-Run Hydration Cache
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-HYDRATE-SKIP-PATH Broken Path Skip
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-HYDRATE-SKIP-IDX Broken Index Skip
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-HYDRATE-ALIGN Hydrated Row Alignment
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-CHUNK-CONTRACT Chunk Runtime Contract
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-HYDRATE-NO-MUTATE Hydration Source Integrity
    STATEMENT: Retriever shall hydrate each fused ranked row into a Chunk object before writing request-time document context into SuperPrompt.
    GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-HYDRATE-PATH
    8.2. Hydration Source Path
    UID: GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-HYDRATE-PATH
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-HYDRATE Hydration Requirement
    STATEMENT: Hydration shall resolve metadata path relative to the configured document root.
    GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-HYDRATE-INDEX
    8.3. Hydration Chunk Index
    UID: GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-HYDRATE-INDEX
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-HYDRATE Hydration Requirement
    STATEMENT: Hydration shall require chunk_idx metadata and shall select the corresponding reconstructed chunk.
    GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-HYDRATE-SAME-CONFIG
    8.4. Hydration Chunking Consistency
    UID: GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-HYDRATE-SAME-CONFIG
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-HYDRATE Hydration Requirement
    STATEMENT: Hydration shall use the same effective chunk size and overlap as ingestion for the source-version being reconstructed.
    GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-HYDRATE-CACHE
    8.5. Per-Run Hydration Cache
    UID: GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-HYDRATE-CACHE
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-HYDRATE Hydration Requirement
    STATEMENT: Hydration shall cache source text and reconstructed chunk lists per source path within one retrieval run to avoid repeated reads and splits.
    GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-HYDRATE-SKIP-PATH
    8.6. Broken Path Skip
    UID: GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-HYDRATE-SKIP-PATH
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-HYDRATE Hydration Requirement
    STATEMENT: A ranked row with missing path metadata or a missing raw source file shall be skipped rather than crashing hydration of all other rows.
    GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-HYDRATE-SKIP-IDX
    8.7. Broken Index Skip
    UID: GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-HYDRATE-SKIP-IDX
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-HYDRATE Hydration Requirement
    STATEMENT: A ranked row with absent, negative, or out-of-range chunk index shall be skipped rather than becoming a fabricated chunk.
    GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-HYDRATE-ALIGN
    8.8. Hydrated Row Alignment
    UID: GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-HYDRATE-ALIGN
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-HYDRATE Hydration Requirement
    RELATIONS (Child):
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-AC-HYDRATE AC: Hydration
    STATEMENT: The list of valid ranked rows and hydrated Chunk objects shall remain one-to-one and order-aligned.
    GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-CHUNK-CONTRACT
    8.9. Chunk Runtime Contract
    UID: GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-CHUNK-CONTRACT
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-HYDRATE Hydration Requirement
    STATEMENT: A hydrated Chunk shall contain stable identifier, project-relative source, snippet, reconstructed source span, and copied metadata.
    GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-HYDRATE-NO-MUTATE
    8.10. Hydration Source Integrity
    UID: GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-HYDRATE-NO-MUTATE
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-HYDRATE Hydration Requirement
    STATEMENT: Hydration shall read source files and shall not modify the raw project document or persistent vector metadata.

    9. 9. Retrieval-Stage SuperPrompt Write-Back

    STATEMENT: The retrieval stage establishes both a retrieval view and an explicit A3-ready passthrough view.
    GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-WRITE
    9.1. Retrieval Write-Back
    UID: GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-WRITE
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-ROOT Document Retrieval and Reranking Scope
    RELATIONS (Child):
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-BASE Base Context Chunks
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-VIEW Retrieval View
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-PASSTHROUGH A3-Ready Passthrough View
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-FINAL-IDS Retrieval Final IDs
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-STAGE Retrieval Lifecycle
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-DIAG Retrieval Passthrough Diagnostics
    STATEMENT: Retriever shall write valid hydrated chunks and ordered ranking state into the same SuperPrompt.
    GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-BASE
    9.2. Base Context Chunks
    UID: GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-BASE
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-WRITE Retrieval Write-Back
    STATEMENT: sp.base_context_chunks shall contain hydrated chunks in fused retrieval order.
    GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-VIEW
    9.3. Retrieval View
    UID: GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-VIEW
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-WRITE Retrieval Write-Back
    STATEMENT: sp.views_by_stage["retrieval"] shall contain ordered triples of chunk identifier, retrieval fused score, and SELECTED status.
    GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-PASSTHROUGH
    9.4. A3-Ready Passthrough View
    UID: GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-PASSTHROUGH
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-WRITE Retrieval Write-Back
    RELATIONS (Child):
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-PASSTHROUGH-NOT-RERANK Passthrough Truthfulness
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-AC-PASSTHROUGH AC: Retrieval Passthrough
    STATEMENT: Retrieval shall initialize sp.views_by_stage["reranked"] as a copy of retrieval ordering and scores so A3 can run when real ColBERT reranking is not executed.
    GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-PASSTHROUGH-NOT-RERANK
    9.5. Passthrough Truthfulness
    UID: GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-PASSTHROUGH-NOT-RERANK
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-PASSTHROUGH A3-Ready Passthrough View
    STATEMENT: The retrieval-created reranked view shall be marked as passthrough and shall not be represented as a completed ColBERT ranking.
    GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-FINAL-IDS
    9.6. Retrieval Final IDs
    UID: GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-FINAL-IDS
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-WRITE Retrieval Write-Back
    STATEMENT: sp.final_selection_ids shall equal the ordered identifiers of the valid hydrated retrieval rows.
    GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-STAGE
    9.7. Retrieval Lifecycle
    UID: GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-STAGE
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-WRITE Retrieval Write-Back
    STATEMENT: After successful retrieval write-back, sp.stage shall equal retrieval and history_of_stages shall receive retrieval.
    GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-DIAG
    9.8. Retrieval Passthrough Diagnostics
    UID: GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-DIAG
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-WRITE Retrieval Write-Back
    STATEMENT: SuperPrompt extras shall record the A3-ready source, candidate count, and reranked passthrough state.

    10. 10. Optional ColBERT Reranking Input and Cleaning

    STATEMENT: Reranker consumes retrieval candidates and may bypass or execute a ColBERT scoring path.
    GHOST-DOC-RERANK-ROOT
    10.1. Reranker Capability
    UID: GHOST-DOC-RERANK-ROOT
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-ROOT Document Retrieval and Reranking Scope
    RELATIONS (Child):
    • GHOST-DOC-RERANK-PRECONDITION Reranker Preconditions
    • GHOST-DOC-RERANK-QUERY Reranker Query Projection
    • GHOST-DOC-RERANK-CANDIDATE-CAP Reranker Candidate Cap
    • GHOST-DOC-RERANK-MODEL Current ColBERT Model
    • GHOST-DOC-RERANK-DEVICE Reranker Device Governance
    • GHOST-DOC-RERANK-CLEAN-DYNAMIC Dynamic Cleaning Only
    • GHOST-DOC-RERANK-NO-VALID No Valid Rerank Candidates
    • GHOST-DOC-RERANK-ENCODE ColBERT Encoding
    • GHOST-DOC-RERANK-RRF2 Second Weighted RRF
    • GHOST-DOC-RERANK-BYPASS ColBERT Bypass
    • GHOST-DOC-RERANK-VIEW Reranked View
    • GHOST-DOC-RERANK-IDS Reranked Final IDs
    • GHOST-DOC-RERANK-STAGE Reranker Lifecycle
    • GHOST-DOC-RERANK-BASE-PRESERVE Base Chunk Preservation
    STATEMENT: Reranker shall optionally rescore and reorder the bounded document candidates already stored by Retrieval.
    GHOST-DOC-RERANK-PRECONDITION
    10.2. Reranker Preconditions
    UID: GHOST-DOC-RERANK-PRECONDITION
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-DOC-RERANK-ROOT Reranker Capability
    STATEMENT: Reranker shall require a non-empty retrieval view and non-empty base_context_chunks.
    GHOST-DOC-RERANK-QUERY
    10.3. Reranker Query Projection
    UID: GHOST-DOC-RERANK-QUERY
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-DOC-RERANK-ROOT Reranker Capability
    STATEMENT: Real reranking shall rebuild the current query text through SuperPromptProjector and split it with the configured query-window contract.
    GHOST-DOC-RERANK-CANDIDATE-CAP
    10.4. Reranker Candidate Cap
    UID: GHOST-DOC-RERANK-CANDIDATE-CAP
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-DOC-RERANK-ROOT Reranker Capability
    STATEMENT: Real or bypass reranking shall process no more than the effective reranker top_k candidates; the current constructor baseline shall be 50.
    GHOST-DOC-RERANK-MODEL
    10.5. Current ColBERT Model
    UID: GHOST-DOC-RERANK-MODEL
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-DOC-RERANK-ROOT Reranker Capability
    STATEMENT: The current reranker model baseline shall be lightonai/GTE-ModernColBERT-v1 until changed by approved configuration.
    GHOST-DOC-RERANK-DEVICE
    10.6. Reranker Device Governance
    UID: GHOST-DOC-RERANK-DEVICE
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-DOC-RERANK-ROOT Reranker Capability
    STATEMENT: The reranker runtime device shall be configurable and shall be honored by model initialization.

    The current constructor default of cpu shall not be claimed as enforced when the backend model is created without applying the device argument.
    GHOST-DOC-RERANK-CLEAN-DYNAMIC
    10.7. Dynamic Cleaning Only
    UID: GHOST-DOC-RERANK-CLEAN-DYNAMIC
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-DOC-RERANK-ROOT Reranker Capability
    RELATIONS (Child):
    • GHOST-DOC-RERANK-CLEAN-FRONT Front-Matter Cleaning
    • GHOST-DOC-RERANK-CLEAN-META Metadata-Line Cleaning
    • GHOST-DOC-RERANK-CLEAN-ARTIFACT Prompt-Artifact Cleaning
    • GHOST-DOC-RERANK-CLEAN-EMPTY Empty Cleaned Candidate
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-AC-CLEAN AC: Non-Mutating Cleaning
    STATEMENT: Reranker shall clean a temporary scoring copy of each chunk text and shall not alter the stored or hydrated canonical snippet.
    GHOST-DOC-RERANK-CLEAN-FRONT
    10.8. Front-Matter Cleaning
    UID: GHOST-DOC-RERANK-CLEAN-FRONT
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-DOC-RERANK-CLEAN-DYNAMIC Dynamic Cleaning Only
    STATEMENT: Reranker cleaning shall remove an initial delimited front-matter block when present.
    GHOST-DOC-RERANK-CLEAN-META
    10.9. Metadata-Line Cleaning
    UID: GHOST-DOC-RERANK-CLEAN-META
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-DOC-RERANK-CLEAN-DYNAMIC Dynamic Cleaning Only
    STATEMENT: Reranker cleaning shall remove configured line-level metadata keys such as title, author, version, updated, tags, dates, and description.
    GHOST-DOC-RERANK-CLEAN-ARTIFACT
    10.10. Prompt-Artifact Cleaning
    UID: GHOST-DOC-RERANK-CLEAN-ARTIFACT
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-DOC-RERANK-CLEAN-DYNAMIC Dynamic Cleaning Only
    STATEMENT: Reranker cleaning shall remove code-fence marker lines, @@meta lines, and recognized END_OF_PROMPT marker lines while preserving normal content.
    GHOST-DOC-RERANK-CLEAN-EMPTY
    10.11. Empty Cleaned Candidate
    UID: GHOST-DOC-RERANK-CLEAN-EMPTY
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-DOC-RERANK-CLEAN-DYNAMIC Dynamic Cleaning Only
    STATEMENT: A candidate whose cleaned scoring text is empty shall be excluded from ColBERT scoring.
    GHOST-DOC-RERANK-NO-VALID
    10.12. No Valid Rerank Candidates
    UID: GHOST-DOC-RERANK-NO-VALID
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-DOC-RERANK-ROOT Reranker Capability
    STATEMENT: Real reranking shall fail explicitly when no candidate can be prepared for ColBERT scoring.

    11. 11. ColBERT Scoring and Second Fusion

    STATEMENT: ColBERT scores the same bounded candidate documents for every query piece and the result is fused with retrieval order.
    GHOST-DOC-RERANK-ENCODE
    11.1. ColBERT Encoding
    UID: GHOST-DOC-RERANK-ENCODE
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-DOC-RERANK-ROOT Reranker Capability
    RELATIONS (Child):
    • GHOST-DOC-RERANK-DOCS-PER-QUERY Candidate Set per Query Piece
    • GHOST-DOC-RERANK-MEAN ColBERT Piece Aggregation
    • GHOST-DOC-RERANK-COLBERT-SORT ColBERT Deterministic Sort
    STATEMENT: Real reranking shall encode query pieces as queries and the cleaned candidate snippets as documents using the configured ColBERT model.
    GHOST-DOC-RERANK-DOCS-PER-QUERY
    11.2. Candidate Set per Query Piece
    UID: GHOST-DOC-RERANK-DOCS-PER-QUERY
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-DOC-RERANK-ENCODE ColBERT Encoding
    STATEMENT: Every query piece shall be scored against the same ordered valid candidate identifier and document list.
    GHOST-DOC-RERANK-MEAN
    11.3. ColBERT Piece Aggregation
    UID: GHOST-DOC-RERANK-MEAN
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-DOC-RERANK-ENCODE ColBERT Encoding
    STATEMENT: For each chunk identifier, ColBERT scores returned across query pieces shall be aggregated by arithmetic mean.
    GHOST-DOC-RERANK-COLBERT-SORT
    11.4. ColBERT Deterministic Sort
    UID: GHOST-DOC-RERANK-COLBERT-SORT
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-DOC-RERANK-ENCODE ColBERT Encoding
    STATEMENT: Aggregated ColBERT rows shall be ordered by descending mean score and ascending chunk identifier for ties.
    GHOST-DOC-RERANK-RRF2
    11.5. Second Weighted RRF
    UID: GHOST-DOC-RERANK-RRF2
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-DOC-RERANK-ROOT Reranker Capability
    RELATIONS (Child):
    • GHOST-DOC-RERANK-WEIGHTS Current Reranker Fusion Weights
    • GHOST-DOC-RERANK-META-PRESERVE Retrieval Metadata Preservation
    • GHOST-DOC-RERANK-META-PROJECT Reranker Metadata Projection
    • GHOST-DOC-RERANK-WRITE-META Chunk Metadata Write-Back
    • GHOST-DOC-RERANK-FINAL-SORT Final Rerank Order
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-AC-COLBERT AC: ColBERT Reranking
    STATEMENT: Reranker shall fuse the original retrieval ranking with the ColBERT ranking through the same neutral weighted RRF helper.
    GHOST-DOC-RERANK-WEIGHTS
    11.6. Current Reranker Fusion Weights
    UID: GHOST-DOC-RERANK-WEIGHTS
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-DOC-RERANK-RRF2 Second Weighted RRF
    RELATIONS (Child):
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-AC-WEIGHTS AC: Executed Weight Truth
    STATEMENT: The current second-fusion baseline shall weight Retrieval 0.75 and ColBERT 0.25.

    Comments describing equal weighting shall not override the executed values.
    GHOST-DOC-RERANK-META-PRESERVE
    11.7. Retrieval Metadata Preservation
    UID: GHOST-DOC-RERANK-META-PRESERVE
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-DOC-RERANK-RRF2 Second Weighted RRF
    STATEMENT: Before the second fusion overwrites generic rrf_score, the original retrieval fused score shall be preserved under retrieval_rrf_score when available.
    GHOST-DOC-RERANK-META-PROJECT
    11.8. Reranker Metadata Projection
    UID: GHOST-DOC-RERANK-META-PROJECT
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-DOC-RERANK-RRF2 Second Weighted RRF
    STATEMENT: Reranker shall project neutral second-fusion fields to retrieval_score, colbert_score, retrieval_rank, colbert_rank, and rerank_rrf_score.
    GHOST-DOC-RERANK-WRITE-META
    11.9. Chunk Metadata Write-Back
    UID: GHOST-DOC-RERANK-WRITE-META
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-DOC-RERANK-RRF2 Second Weighted RRF
    STATEMENT: Reranker-specific scores and ranks shall be merged into the hydrated chunk metadata for later diagnostic rendering.
    GHOST-DOC-RERANK-FINAL-SORT
    11.10. Final Rerank Order
    UID: GHOST-DOC-RERANK-FINAL-SORT
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-DOC-RERANK-RRF2 Second Weighted RRF
    STATEMENT: The final reranked rows shall be sorted by descending rerank fused score and ascending chunk identifier for ties.

    12. 12. Reranking Bypass and SuperPrompt Write-Back

    STATEMENT: Bypass preserves downstream contracts without claiming model scoring.
    GHOST-DOC-RERANK-BYPASS
    12.1. ColBERT Bypass
    UID: GHOST-DOC-RERANK-BYPASS
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-DOC-RERANK-ROOT Reranker Capability
    RELATIONS (Child):
    • GHOST-DOC-RERANK-BYPASS-META Bypass Metadata
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-AC-RERANK-BYPASS AC: ColBERT Bypass
    STATEMENT: When use_reranking_colbert is false, Reranker shall copy the bounded retrieval order and scores into the reranked stage without calling the ColBERT model.
    GHOST-DOC-RERANK-BYPASS-META
    12.2. Bypass Metadata
    UID: GHOST-DOC-RERANK-BYPASS-META
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-DOC-RERANK-BYPASS ColBERT Bypass
    STATEMENT: Bypass may populate retrieval and ColBERT score/rank aliases from the retrieval order solely to preserve downstream metadata shape; diagnostics shall identify the bypass.
    GHOST-DOC-RERANK-VIEW
    12.3. Reranked View
    UID: GHOST-DOC-RERANK-VIEW
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-DOC-RERANK-ROOT Reranker Capability
    STATEMENT: sp.views_by_stage["reranked"] shall contain ordered chunk identifier, effective rerank score, and SELECTED status for every retained candidate.
    GHOST-DOC-RERANK-IDS
    12.4. Reranked Final IDs
    UID: GHOST-DOC-RERANK-IDS
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-DOC-RERANK-ROOT Reranker Capability
    STATEMENT: sp.final_selection_ids shall equal the ordered identifiers in the reranked view.
    GHOST-DOC-RERANK-STAGE
    12.5. Reranker Lifecycle
    UID: GHOST-DOC-RERANK-STAGE
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-DOC-RERANK-ROOT Reranker Capability
    STATEMENT: After real or bypass reranking, sp.stage shall equal reranked and history_of_stages shall receive reranked.
    GHOST-DOC-RERANK-BASE-PRESERVE
    12.6. Base Chunk Preservation
    UID: GHOST-DOC-RERANK-BASE-PRESERVE
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-DOC-RERANK-ROOT Reranker Capability
    STATEMENT: Reranker shall not replace base_context_chunks; it shall reorder candidates through views and selection identifiers while enriching metadata only.

    13. 13. Configuration, Error Handling, and Observability

    STATEMENT: Retrieval/reranking parameters shall migrate to validated JSON configuration and effective behavior shall remain visible.
    GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-CONFIG-JSON
    13.1. JSON Configuration Target
    UID: GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-CONFIG-JSON
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-ROOT Document Retrieval and Reranking Scope
    RELATIONS (Child):
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-CONFIG-FIELDS Retrieval Configuration Fields
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-CONFIG-UNUSED Unused Current Runtime Keys
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-CONFIG-WEIGHTS Weight Validation
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-CONFIG-LIMITS Limit Validation
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-AC-CONFIG AC: JSON Parameter Migration
    STATEMENT: Retrieval and reranking tuning parameters shall be migrated to versioned runtime_config.json.
    GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-CONFIG-FIELDS
    13.2. Retrieval Configuration Fields
    UID: GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-CONFIG-FIELDS
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-CONFIG-JSON JSON Configuration Target
    STATEMENT: The configuration target shall include query chunk size/overlap, dense model, dense p value, dense candidate limit, embedding floor, SPLADE enablement/model/device/p value, first RRF constant/weights, hydration chunk parameters, reranker model/device/candidate cap, ColBERT enablement, and second RRF constant/weights.
    GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-CONFIG-UNUSED
    13.3. Unused Current Runtime Keys
    UID: GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-CONFIG-UNUSED
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-CONFIG-JSON JSON Configuration Target
    STATEMENT: runtime_config document_retrieval keys that are not consumed by the active code, including semantic_stage_max_total_chunks or max_document_chunks_for_a3 where not wired, shall not be described as enforced runtime limits.
    GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-CONFIG-WEIGHTS
    13.4. Weight Validation
    UID: GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-CONFIG-WEIGHTS
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-CONFIG-JSON JSON Configuration Target
    STATEMENT: Configured fusion weights shall be finite and non-negative and at least one branch weight shall be positive.
    GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-CONFIG-LIMITS
    13.5. Limit Validation
    UID: GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-CONFIG-LIMITS
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-CONFIG-JSON JSON Configuration Target
    STATEMENT: Configured candidate limits, p values, RRF constants, and query chunk size shall be positive; overlap shall satisfy the splitter contract.
    GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-EFFECTIVE
    13.6. Effective Retrieval Diagnostics
    UID: GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-EFFECTIVE
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-ROOT Document Retrieval and Reranking Scope
    STATEMENT: The run shall expose effective query-piece count, dense count, floor-survivor count, sparse mode/count, fusion weights, hydrated count, reranker mode/count, and model identifiers.
    GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-ERROR
    13.7. Retrieval Error Integrity
    UID: GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-ERROR
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-ROOT Document Retrieval and Reranking Scope
    STATEMENT: Missing dense project stores, invalid vector shapes, incompatible dimensions, sparse candidate misalignment, empty reranker preparation, or invalid configuration shall not produce fabricated successful rankings.
    GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-OPTIONAL-ERROR
    13.8. Optional Branch Failure Boundary
    UID: GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-OPTIONAL-ERROR
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-ROOT Document Retrieval and Reranking Scope
    STATEMENT: Only documented optional-branch failures may degrade to a fallback; mandatory dense retrieval and hydration-contract failures shall remain observable.
    GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-LOG-CONTENT
    13.9. Retrieval Logging Content
    UID: GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-LOG-CONTENT
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-ROOT Document Retrieval and Reranking Scope
    STATEMENT: Normal logs shall expose stage status, counts, effective modes, model identifiers, and latency without dumping full project evidence by default.

    14. 14. Traceability and Acceptance Criteria

    STATEMENT: These criteria verify the complete retrieval/reranking contract before A3.
    GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-CODE-OWNERSHIP
    14.1. Retrieval Code Ownership
    UID: GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-CODE-OWNERSHIP
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-ROOT Document Retrieval and Reranking Scope
    RELATIONS (Child):
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-AC-SYNC AC: Requirement-Code Synchronization
    STATEMENT: Retriever, RetrieverEmb, RetrieverSplade, rrf_merge, smart_query_splitter, Chunk, DocScore compatibility, and Reranker shall map to the deepest applicable UIDs in this document.
    GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-SIBLING
    14.2. Sibling Ownership Boundary
    UID: GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-SIBLING
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-ROOT Document Retrieval and Reranking Scope
    STATEMENT: Document ingestion shall remain owned by 41 and A3/A4 behavior shall remain owned by 43.
    GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-AC-DENSE
    14.3. AC: Dense Ranking
    UID: GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-AC-DENSE
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-DENSE-FIRST Dense First-Pass Retrieval
    STATEMENT: Given a valid project store and query pieces, dense retrieval shall return at most top_k rows ordered by p-norm aggregated cosine score with deterministic tie resolution.
    GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-AC-FLOOR
    14.4. AC: Hard Floor
    UID: GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-AC-FLOOR
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-EMB-FLOOR Hard Dense Similarity Floor
    STATEMENT: Given a positive hard embedding floor, every dense row entering SPLADE/fusion shall have dense score greater than or equal to that floor.
    GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-AC-SPARSE
    14.5. AC: Dense-Bounded SPLADE
    UID: GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-AC-SPARSE
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-SPARSE-CANDIDATES Dense-Bounded Sparse Candidates
    STATEMENT: Given SPLADE enabled, every sparse-scored identifier shall belong to the dense survivor set and every dense survivor shall be present in the sparse store or the branch shall fail explicitly.
    GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-AC-SPARSE-BYPASS
    14.6. AC: SPLADE Bypass
    UID: GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-AC-SPARSE-BYPASS
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-SPARSE-BYPASS SPLADE Bypass Contract
    STATEMENT: Given SPLADE disabled, retrieval shall complete using dense duplication as the second fusion input and shall expose bypass status.
    GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-AC-RRF1
    14.7. AC: First Fusion
    UID: GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-AC-RRF1
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-RRF1 Retrieval RRF Fusion
    STATEMENT: Given two ranked inputs and configured weights, fused scores and order shall match deterministic weighted RRF with the configured rank constant.
    GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-AC-HYDRATE
    14.8. AC: Hydration
    UID: GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-AC-HYDRATE
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-HYDRATE-ALIGN Hydrated Row Alignment
    STATEMENT: Every row written to the retrieval view shall have a corresponding hydrated Chunk; broken rows shall be omitted from both aligned outputs.
    GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-AC-PASSTHROUGH
    14.9. AC: Retrieval Passthrough
    UID: GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-AC-PASSTHROUGH
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-PASSTHROUGH A3-Ready Passthrough View
    STATEMENT: After retrieval without real reranking, the reranked view shall equal the retrieval view and diagnostics shall identify retrieval_passthrough.
    GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-AC-COLBERT
    14.10. AC: ColBERT Reranking
    UID: GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-AC-COLBERT
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-DOC-RERANK-RRF2 Second Weighted RRF
    STATEMENT: Given real reranking enabled and valid candidates, ColBERT mean scores shall be fused with retrieval ranking using the configured second-fusion values and shall produce the reranked view.
    GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-AC-RERANK-BYPASS
    14.11. AC: ColBERT Bypass
    UID: GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-AC-RERANK-BYPASS
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-DOC-RERANK-BYPASS ColBERT Bypass
    STATEMENT: Given ColBERT disabled, no ColBERT model call shall occur and retrieval order shall be copied into the reranked stage.
    GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-AC-CLEAN
    14.12. AC: Non-Mutating Cleaning
    UID: GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-AC-CLEAN
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-DOC-RERANK-CLEAN-DYNAMIC Dynamic Cleaning Only
    STATEMENT: Reranker cleaning shall alter only the temporary scoring text; the corresponding base_context_chunks snippets shall remain unchanged.
    GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-AC-WEIGHTS
    14.13. AC: Executed Weight Truth
    UID: GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-AC-WEIGHTS
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-DOC-RERANK-WEIGHTS Current Reranker Fusion Weights
    STATEMENT: The effective retrieval and reranking weights reported for the current baseline shall be 0.75/0.25 unless validated JSON configuration overrides them in implemented code.
    GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-AC-CONFIG
    14.14. AC: JSON Parameter Migration
    UID: GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-AC-CONFIG
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-CONFIG-JSON JSON Configuration Target
    STATEMENT: After configuration migration, changing a retrieval limit, p value, RRF constant, branch weight, model, device, or query window shall require only validated JSON change and shall be reflected in diagnostics.
    GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-AC-SYNC
    14.15. AC: Requirement-Code Synchronization
    UID: GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-AC-SYNC
    STATUS: Draft
    RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-CODE-OWNERSHIP Retrieval Code Ownership
    STATEMENT: A retrieval baseline shall not be accepted when query routing, branch scope, fusion weights, hydration semantics, passthrough meaning, or reranking write-back contradicts this document.
    Built with StrictDoc 0.25.0
